description_long: This house features a modern, angular design with a prominent multi-faceted roof and a mix of flat and sloped surfaces. The exterior is clad in a light tan material, likely wood or composite siding, with large rectangular windows distributed asymmetrically across both floors. The structure includes two distinct enclosed porch or sunroom areas with extensive glass paneling, one of which is topped with a pergola-style open roof. The main entryway is centrally located and flanked by garage doors, suggesting an integrated garage. The house's overall footprint is irregular, with protruding sections and recessed corners, creating visual interest and maximizing natural light. The roof overhangs and window placements indicate a design focused on both aesthetics and functionality.
description: A modern, angular two-story house with light tan siding, large windows, and two glass-enclosed porch areas, one featuring a pergola roof. The design includes an integrated garage and an irregular, multi-faceted roofline.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway, integrated garage doors, and two glass-enclosed porch areas, one with a pergola roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the sunroom with glass walls and the sloped roof sections, as well as a secondary entry door.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a more solid facade with smaller, asymmetrically placed windows and minimal architectural detailing.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the second glass-enclosed porch area and the angular roof structure, with visible window arrangements.
attribute_entryway_design: Central entryway with double doors, flanked by garage doors and sheltered by a modest overhang; no prominent porch or columns.
